expectations.evidence: ASSESSMENT-REPORT.md section 1 'Project Profile' explicitly classifies the project as a TypeScript software project: Language=TypeScript, Runtime=Node.js/Bun, UI Framework=React+Ink (terminal), ~1,888 TypeScript files. It lists architectural modules (tools, commands, hooks, components, services, utils, state, types) with file counts.

The report covers Controllability (section 2) and Observability (section 3) as top-level assessment sections, but it does NOT use the E1-E4, O1-O4, V1-V3 dimension codes at all. There is NO Verification dimension section or assessment. Only 2 of the 3 expected dimensions (controllability, observability, verification) are covered, and none of the 11 individual sub-dimensions are labeled.

ASSESSMENT-REPORT.md explicitly identifies the gap in the Controllability section: 'No test infrastructure | CRITICAL | Zero test files exist. No test runner, no test configuration, no test helpers.' The Project Profile table also lists 'Test Runner | None'. The Risk Assessment notes 'CI pipeline cannot run tests (no test infra) | CERTAIN'.

ASSESSMENT-REPORT.md section 3 'Existing Observability Assessment' lists seven existing telemetry/analytics utilities: startup profiler (src/utils/startupProfiler.ts), profiler base (src/utils/profilerBase.ts), debug logging (src/utils/debug.ts), cost tracker (src/cost-tracker.ts), diagnostic tracking (src/services/diagnosticTracking.ts), analytics via Statsig (src/services/analytics/), and internal logging (src/services/internalLogging.ts).

14 executable shell scripts plus ci-pipeline.yml exist in the outputs directory: scaffold-controllability.sh, scaffold-observability.sh, scaffold-all.sh, lint-import-direction.sh, lint-export-counts.sh, analyze-deps.sh, validate-tool-signatures.sh, run-all-gates.sh, instrument-tool-lifecycle.sh, trace-query-pipeline.sh, audit-state-mutations.sh, collect-metrics.sh, verify-scaffolding.sh, ci-pipeline.yml. All .sh files have executable permissions.

Scripts reference .ts/.tsx file extensions and TypeScript-specific code patterns (buildTool(), Zod schemas, React/Ink components) but do NOT reference npm, node, npx, tsx, bun, tsc, or any TypeScript toolchain commands. All scripts are pure bash+grep/awk/sed with no Node.js tooling invocations. The CI pipeline (ci-pipeline.yml) sets up Python but does not set up Node.js/npm at all. No script installs or invokes any TypeScript-specific tooling like madge, dependency-cruiser, or tsc.
